title: The Pilgrim
content:
Put by the sun my joyful soul,
    We are for darkness that is whole;

    Put by the wine, now for long years
    We must be thirsty with salt

title: November
content:
As I walk the misty hill
    All is languid, fogged, and still;
    Not a note of any bird
    Nor any motion's hint is heard,
    Save from s

title: The Philosopher's Oration.
content:
(From 'A Faun's Holiday')


    Meanwhile, though nations in distress
    Cower at a comet's loveliness
    Shaken across the midnight sky;

title: The Stranger
content:
Never am I so alone
     As when I walk among the crowd -
    Blurred masks of stern or grinning stone,
     Unmeaning eyes and voices loud.

title: Fulfilment.
content:
Was there love once? I have forgotten her.
    Was there grief once? grief yet is mine.
    Other loves I have, men rough, but men who stir

title: Seventeen
content:

title: The Assault
content:
The beating of the guns grows louder.
    'Not long, boys, now'.
    My heart burns whiter, fearfuller, prouder.

title: To ---
content:

title: The Sprig Of Lime
content:

title: O Nightingale My Heart
content:

title: The Prophetic Bard's Oration
content:
(From 'A Faun's Holiday')

    'Be warned! I feel the world grow old,
    And off Olympus fades the gold
    Of the simple passionate sun;

title: The Tower
content:

    The moon floated, drifting through high vaporous woofs.

content:
(From 'A Faun's Holiday')

title: Night Rhapsody
content:
How beautiful it is to wake at night,
    When over all there reigns the ultimate spell
    Of complete silence, darkness absolute,
    To feel
